events.title: Funny Bunny Egg Hunt
events.shortSummary: A spring egg hunt for kids, with advance registration and a White Plains High School rain location.
events.fullDescription:
White Plains Recreation & Parks has the 2026 Funny Bunny Egg Hunt on Saturday, March 28 from 9:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m.

If the weather doesn't cooperate, it moves to White Plains High School. Check the city page first for registration and day-of details.
events.locationName: White Plains High School North Gym
events.locationAddress: White Plains High School, White Plains, NY
events.organizer: City of White Plains Recreation & Parks

events.title: Common Council Meeting
events.shortSummary: A public Common Council meeting at City Hall.
events.fullDescription:
The White Plains city calendar lists this Common Council meeting for Monday, April 6, 2026 at 7:00 p.m. in the Common Council Chamber on the second floor of City Hall.

If you want to keep up with local decisions, this is a good one to have on your radar.
events.locationAddress: 255 Main Street, 2nd Floor, White Plains, NY 10601

events.title: STEAM Saturday
events.shortSummary: Hands-on STEAM time for kids in grades 4 through 8 at the White Plains Public Library.
events.fullDescription:
STEAM Saturday is set for Saturday, April 4, 2026 from 12:00 p.m. to 1:00 p.m. in Galaxy Hall at the White Plains Public Library.

The library says it's for kids in grades 4 through 8, and tickets are handed out at the Compass Desk in the Trove starting 30 minutes before the program.
events.locationName: White Plains Public Library, Galaxy Hall

events.title: "Energize U" Energy Efficiency Workshop
events.shortSummary: A workshop on lowering energy bills, with dinner and a free conservation kit.
events.fullDescription:
This White Plains Public Library workshop is on Monday, April 6, 2026 from 5:30 p.m. to 7:30 p.m. in the Auditorium.

Dinner starts at 5:30 p.m., the presentation starts at 6:00 p.m., and the library says registration is required. Each person also gets a free energy conservation kit.

events.title: English Conversation Group
events.shortSummary: A free drop-in group for adults who want to practice everyday English.
events.fullDescription:
The White Plains Public Library is holding this conversation group on Thursday, April 9, 2026 from 2:00 p.m. to 3:00 p.m. in the Community Room.

It's meant to help adults practice everyday English in a relaxed setting. No registration is required.
events.locationName: White Plains Public Library Community Room

events.title: Catch Me If You Can
events.shortSummary: A spring run of the musical at the White Plains Performing Arts Center.
events.fullDescription:
White Plains Performing Arts Center is running Catch Me If You Can from April 10 through May 3, 2026.

According to the theater, tickets range from $53 to $71. Check the show page for performance dates and seat availability.
events.locationAddress: 11 City Place, 3rd Floor, White Plains, NY 10601

events.title: Downtown White Plains Wing Walk 2026
events.shortSummary: A ticketed wing tasting across downtown White Plains, with voting and stops at local restaurants.
events.fullDescription:
Wing Walk 2026 is on Saturday, April 25 from 12:00 p.m. to 5:00 p.m. around downtown White Plains.

You'll visit participating restaurants, try their wings, and vote for your favorite. The BID page has ticket info and the full list of stops.
events.locationAddress: Multiple restaurants in downtown White Plains, NY 10601

events.title: Downtown White Plains Rock the Block 2026
events.shortSummary: A free downtown street series with live music, food, games, and room to hang out.
events.fullDescription:
Rock the Block is back on four Wednesdays in 2026: May 20, June 17, July 15, and August 19, from 5:30 p.m. to 8:30 p.m.

The BID turns part of Mamaroneck Avenue into a walkable stretch with music, outdoor dining, mini golf, lawn games, and family activities.
events.locationAddress: Mamaroneck Avenue between Maple Avenue and East Post Road, White Plains, NY 10601

events.title: WPCNA Neighborhood Workshop
events.shortSummary: Workshop materials from WPCNA's first annual session for people starting or reviving neighborhood associations.
events.fullDescription:
These materials came from WPCNA's first annual neighborhood workshop on March 11, 2025.

They're still useful if you're trying to start a neighborhood association, bring one back to life, or figure out what that work looks like in White Plains.
events.organizer: White Plains Council of Neighborhood Associations

events.title: Final Vision Zero Public Meeting
events.shortSummary: A public meeting on White Plains street safety and the next steps in the Vision Zero plan.
events.fullDescription:
This Vision Zero public meeting was held on Thursday, April 24, 2025 from 6:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m. at the White Plains Public Library.

The discussion focused on safety recommendations, public feedback, and the next phase of the city's Vision Zero Action Plan.

events.title: Arbor Day in White Plains
events.shortSummary: An Arbor Day event shared through the WPCNA archive and kept here with its flyer.
events.fullDescription:
WPCNA archived this Arbor Day flyer for Friday, April 25, 2025.

The flyer is still here if you want to look back at the event or keep a record of past White Plains community programming.

events.title: Downtown White Plains Wing Walk 2025
events.shortSummary: A downtown wing tasting with voting, timed entry, and stops at restaurants around White Plains.
events.fullDescription:
Wing Walk 2025 took place on Saturday, April 26 from noon to 5:00 p.m. across downtown White Plains.

The BID page described a full afternoon of tasting and voting, with ticketed entry and participating restaurants across downtown.
events.locationAddress: Multiple restaurants in downtown White Plains, NY 10601

events.title: Rock the Block 2025
events.shortSummary: A downtown summer series with live music, food, and family activities on Mamaroneck Avenue.
events.fullDescription:
The 2025 Rock the Block series ran from May through August on Mamaroneck Avenue.

It brought live music, outdoor dining, games, and a lot of foot traffic into downtown White Plains over the summer.
events.locationAddress: Mamaroneck Avenue between Maple Avenue and East Post Road, White Plains, NY 10601

events.title: Block-toberfest
events.shortSummary: A free fall block party with music, games, vendors, and family activities downtown.
events.fullDescription:
Block-toberfest took place on Sunday, October 26, 2025 from 1:00 p.m. to 6:00 p.m. on Mamaroneck Avenue.

The BID used it as a fall downtown block party after its usual Oktoberfest plans changed, with music, food, games, and seasonal activities.
events.locationAddress: Mamaroneck Avenue between Maple Avenue and East Post Road, White Plains, NY 10601

events.title: Downtown White Plains Holiday Market
events.shortSummary: An indoor holiday market with local vendors in the former Barnes & Noble space at City Center.
events.fullDescription:
The Downtown White Plains Holiday Market ran from December 5 through December 14, 2025, from 11:00 a.m. to 7:00 p.m. each day.

It brought local vendors into the former Barnes & Noble space at City Center for a stretch of holiday shopping downtown.
events.locationName: White Plains City Center, former Barnes & Noble space
events.locationAddress: White Plains City Center, White Plains, NY 10601

events.title: Vision Zero Public Meeting
events.shortSummary: A public meeting about street safety in White Plains and the Vision Zero Action Plan.
events.fullDescription:
This Vision Zero public meeting was held on Wednesday, October 23, 2024 from 6:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m. at the White Plains Public Library Auditorium.

The goal was to share information, hear questions, and get public feedback on the city's street-safety work.

events.title: Vision Zero Action Plan Stakeholder Meeting
events.shortSummary: A stakeholder meeting on White Plains street safety, plan goals, and early findings.
events.fullDescription:
This stakeholder meeting took place on Tuesday, September 24, 2024 from 6:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m. in the Common Council Chamber at City Hall.

The agenda covered plan goals, timing, early analysis, and feedback on safety issues around White Plains.
events.locationAddress: 255 Main Street, 2nd Floor, White Plains, NY 10601

events.title: Downtown Revitalization Initiative Public Workshop
events.shortSummary: A public workshop on downtown White Plains priorities like open space, public art, and bike lanes.
events.fullDescription:
This public workshop was held on Monday, September 9, 2024 from 6:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m. as part of the Downtown Revitalization Initiative.

People were invited to look at possible downtown projects and weigh in on what mattered most, including public space, art, and bike-friendly improvements.
events.locationAddress: 100 Martine Avenue, 2nd Floor, White Plains, NY 10601
